what_it_does: Implements a durable FIFO commit queue that serializes all repo writes under a commit-to-main/no-branches policy. Producers call enqueue to append a small JSONL record (append or commit kind); a single cross-process exclusive drainer processes items one at a time, stages, commits, advances a persisted cursor, and deadletters failures with gap notices.
observations: Enqueue is lock-free via a single O_APPEND write of a JSONL line; large append_content is spilled to a CAS payload file so the line stays small.
Drain uses fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) on a dedicated lock file to guarantee only one drainer runs across processes.
A persisted cursor tracks consumption offset and is rewritten after every item, successful or failed.
Failed items are written to a deadletter JSONL and a gap notice is emitted; nothing is silently dropped.
Validation is fail-loud at enqueue time; invalid kinds, missing fields, or oversized lines raise CommitQueueError.
The CLI only exposes drain; enqueue is intended as a library call.
No Co-Authored-By trailer is added to commits, per repo law.
standouts: Lock-free enqueue combined with payload spilling to keep queue lines atomic and small.
Single cross-process drainer with cursor-based resume and loud deadlettering.
Treats 'nothing to commit' as a failure rather than a silent no-op.
conventions: Heavy docstring at top states problem, fix, design, and laws.
Atomic file updates via temp+os.replace for cursor.
Type hints on public and private functions.
concerns: The cursor is written after the git commit completes, so a crash between commit and cursor write could reprocess the same item on restart, contradicting the 'never reprocess' claim.
_all_items loads the entire queue file into memory on every drain, which may not scale.
Payload files are never cleaned up after successful application.
There is no retry, backoff, or idempotency mechanism for failed items beyond deadlettering.
The queue file itself is never compacted or rotated.
notes: This file is the repo's concurrency guard for commit-to-main: it funnels concurrent writes into one serialized committer with a durable queue, cursor, and loud failure log.
questions: What process or scheduler is responsible for calling drain?
How are deadletter/gap entries surfaced to an operator?
Is the O_APPEND atomicity guarantee considered sufficient on all target filesystems?
Are there plans to compact or rotate the queue file as it grows?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches the stated purpose, but the crash-safety claim of 'never reprocess' is not fully guaranteed because the cursor is advanced after the git commit, leaving a small window where a crash could cause duplicate application.
intention_signals: The code is trying to eliminate index-lock races from concurrent git add/commit by forcing all writes through a single FIFO drainer, while keeping enqueue cheap, cross-process safe, and observable.
novelty: The combination of O_APPEND atomic JSONL lines plus CAS payload spilling for lock-free cross-process enqueue, paired with a cursor-driven single drainer and integrated deadletter/gap notices.
contribution: A durable FIFO commit serializer with lock-free enqueue, cross-process exclusive drain, cursor-based resume, and loud deadlettering — a keepable mechanism for safe commit-to-main concurrency.
summary_for_embedding: ops/commit_queue.py implements a durable FIFO commit queue that serializes append and commit operations through a single cross-process-locked drainer to prevent concurrent git add/commit races under a commit-to-main policy.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a crash-safe, fail-loud serialization mechanism for concurrent repo writes so that only one commit happens at a time and no failed write is silently dropped.

symbols.does: Exception class raised for malformed enqueue requests.
symbols.description: Subclass of ValueError used to fail loudly when an enqueue item has an invalid kind, missing required fields, or an oversized queue line, ensuring malformed items never reach the drainer.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC ISO timestamp.
symbols.description: Helper used to timestamp queue records, deadletter entries, and gap notices.

symbols.does: Computes filesystem paths for queue artifacts.
symbols.description: Returns a dict of absolute paths for the queue JSONL, cursor, lock file, payload directory, deadletter, and gaps notice file under a given store directory.
Ensures the locks/ and commit_queue_payloads/ directories exist before enqueue or drain operations.

symbols.does: Validates and appends one write request to the queue.
symbols.description: Fail-loud validation; for append kind it atomically spills content to a payload file and writes a small JSONL line via O_APPEND, while for commit kind it writes paths and message; returns the persisted record.
Returns the integer cursor from the cursor file, or 0 if the file is missing or invalid.
Writes the cursor to a temporary file and renames it into place with os.replace for crash safety.
Reads the queue file, skips blank or unparseable lines, and returns a list of record dicts.

symbols.does: Runs a git subprocess in the repo.
symbols.description: Convenience wrapper around subprocess.run that invokes git -C repo with captured stdout/stderr.

symbols.does: Preserves a failed item and emits a notice.
symbols.description: Writes the failed record plus its error to a deadletter JSONL and a gap notice to a gaps JSONL so failures are never silently dropped.

symbols.does: Applies a single queue item and commits it.
symbols.description: For append items it appends the payload to the target file, stages, and commits; for commit items it stages the given paths and commits; returns an (ok, message) tuple and treats an empty working-tree status as a failure.

symbols.does: Processes queued items FIFO under an exclusive cross-process flock.
symbols.description: Acquires an exclusive fcntl lock, reads the queue and cursor, applies each item, advances the cursor after each success or loud failure, deadletters failures, and returns a summary of applied and failed items.
